title: 10 best herbal essential oils for gerd
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for Gerd are natural remedies that have been shown to alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), such as heartburn, acid reflux, and bloating. These oils work by soothing the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing inflammation and easing digestion. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with GERD include peppermint oil, ginger oil, and licorice root oil. Using these oils through massage, inhalation, or internal supplementation can significantly improve quality of life for those suffering from GERD, allowing them to enjoy their favorite foods without discomfort and reducing the need for medication.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. The oil's gentle antacid qualities help neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms like heartburn and bloating. Additionally, lemon balm's carminative properties help ease digestion by reducing gas and flatulence, providing relief from discomfort and discomfort. By using lemon balm essential oils, individuals can experience a significant reduction in GERD symptoms, promoting a healthier digestive system and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax and reduce muscle spasms in the esophagus, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.
Antacid: The essential oil has been shown to have natural antacid properties, helping to neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, discomfort, and pain associated with GERD.
Calming and Sedative: Herbal lemon balm essential oil's calming and sedative effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for acid reflux and GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which makes them ideal for extracting essential oils that can help soothe digestive issues like heartburn and acid reflux.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in citral, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers during full moon for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 days.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis plant material using high-quality steam distillation equipment for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator device within 30 minutes of distillation completion.
Bottle and store the extracted melissa officinalis essential oil in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with Gerd because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophagus and reduce inflammation caused by acid reflux. It also has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with GERD. Additionally, licorice oil can help to relax the stomach muscles, allowing for easier digestion and reducing symptoms of heartburn and bloating. This natural remedy provides relief from chronic acid reflux and allows individuals to manage their symptoms in a safe and non-invasive way.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Herbal licorice essential oil has antacid properties, which means it can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD, such as burning sensations and discomfort.

Soothing properties: Herbal licorice essential oil has a calming effect on the mucous membranes, which can help soothe and protect the esophagus from irritation caused by stomach acid reflux, reducing discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues associated with GERD.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and cut them into small pieces weighing about 200g.
Steam distill the root pieces using a traditional steam distillation apparatus for approximately 4 hours.
Combine the resulting distillate with 10ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil for a ratio of 1:5.
Store the final blend in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat for at least 24 hours to allow for infusion.
Filter the blend through cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper before use to remove any sediment particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with GERD because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the esophageal lining. The aloe vera gel also helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties help eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to GERD symptoms. By using herbal aloe essential oils, individuals can effectively manage their GERD symptoms and enjoy improved digestion and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to GERD symptoms such as heartburn and chest pain.

Soothing and calming property: The soothing and calming effects of aloe vera help alleviate discomfort and irritation in the esophageal lining, providing relief from burning sensations and indigestion associated with GERD.
Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage the esophageal tissue, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
Roots: Aloe roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues, including stomach ulcers and gastritis, making them a potential source for GERD relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality aloe vera leaves from organic farms or your own garden carefully.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in water for 24 hours gently.
Blend the soaked aloe vera mixture with carrier oils like coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 10%.
Allow the blended mixture to sit in a warm dark place for 21 days without disturbance patiently.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the essential oil tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. When applied topically or inhaled, chamomile oil can calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, making it easier to manage the discomfort and pain associated with GERD. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile oil help to relax the muscles in the esophageal sphincter, preventing it from spasming and allowing food to pass through without difficulty.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile oil's soothing and calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for GERD symptoms, and promote relaxation of the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Flowers: Containing apigenin and luteolin, the flowers have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning when dew still present for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or shade drying method at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Steam distill dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with water at a ratio of 1:5 using a steam distillation apparatus for 3 hours and 30 minutes.
Collect the extracted essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a decantation process or centrifuge for 15-20 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil using activated charcoal or diatomaceous earth to remove impurities and improve its color and consistency for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with GERD because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. The oil's anise-like flavor also helps to soothe and relax the digestive system, reducing muscle spasms and easing discomfort. Additionally, fennel essential oil can help to calm bloating and gas, allowing for proper digestion and reducing the risk of stomach acid rising back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Property: Fennel essential oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas, which can contribute to GERD. This property helps reduce discomfort and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Antispasmodic Property: Fennel essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate GERD symptoms such as acid reflux and regurgitation.
Carminative Property: Fennel essential oil helps to expel trapped gas from the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms of bloating, discomfort, and pain associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe digestive issues associated with Gerd.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant contain a compound called anethole, which has natural antacid properties that can provide relief from acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 250 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 500 milliliters of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil from the distillation process using a coffee filter or paper towel.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with Gerd because it has a natural soothing effect on the digestive system, specifically targeting the esophageal sphincter muscle to relax and reduce inflammation. The oil's carvone compound has been shown to ease heartburn symptoms by neutralizing stomach acid and reducing digestion-related discomfort. Additionally, caraway oil's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux, providing relief from chest pain, bloating, and indigestion associated with Gerd.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Caraway essential oil helps relieve spasms in the esophagus, reducing muscle contractions that can exacerbate heartburn and acid reflux.

Anti-inflammatory property: Caraway essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to GERD symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Carminative property: Caraway essential oil is a natural carminative, meaning it helps eliminate gas and alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the caraway plant contain compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: Caraway seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils, including cumic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh carum carvi seeds and thoroughly wash them in distilled water.
Steam distill the washed seeds at a temperature range of 100-150°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight, for up to 12 months.
Use 10-15 drops of the prepared carum carvi essential oil per capsule, filled with carrier oil, and take twice daily before meals for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with GERD because of its soothing and calming properties. It has a gentle anti-inflammatory effect on the esophagus, which can help alleviate heartburn and acid reflux symptoms. Additionally, the oil's natural antacids and acid-reducing compounds can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with GERD.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Damask rose essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of damask rose essential oil can help relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with GERD.

Gastroprotective property: Damask rose essential oil has been found to possess gastroprotective effects, which can help shield the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux and inflammation, thereby reducing symptoms of GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Damask rose roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the esophagus and stomach lining in individuals with GERD.

Flowers: The fragrant flowers of the damask rose plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including GERD, by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn when they are most potent.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a copper still at a temperature of 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol using a decanter.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Bottle the filtered essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for 24 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory essential oils helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the esophageal lining, reducing discomfort and inflammation associated with acid reflux. The oil's digestive benefits also help to calm stomach upset and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and indigestion. Additionally, summer savory essential oil's antioxidant compounds work to neutralize free radicals in the body, further reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax and reduce muscle spasms in the esophagus, which can contribute to heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of GERD such as discomfort, pain, and difficulty swallowing.
Antioxidant property: Neutralizes free radicals that can damage the lining of the esophagus and contribute to chronic inflammation, further reducing symptoms of GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Summer savory roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.
Leaves: The leaves of the summer savory plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to calm acid reflux symptoms and ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh satureja hortensis leaves in the early morning to ensure peak oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 60°c for 12 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush dried satureja hortensis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Steam distill the crushed satureja hortensis powder with 2 cups of water at 100°c for 2 hours to release essential oils.
Filter the resulting essential oil from the distillation mixture using a coffee filter or paper towels, yielding approximately 10ml of high-quality oil after 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander essential oils helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's carminative properties help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion, reducing discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux. Additionally, coriander essential oil has been shown to improve digestion by stimulating digestive enzymes and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, which helps to reduce the production of stomach acid that can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Coriander oil helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can exacerbate GERD symptoms.
Carminative property: The oil has natural carminative properties, which means it helps eliminate gas and bloating from the digestive system, reducing discomfort and symptoms associated with GERD.

Antioxidant property: Coriander essential oil is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to GERD development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Coriander roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD symptoms.
Seeds: Coriander seeds have natural antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve cramps and spasms in the esophagus and stomach that can exacerbate GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest coriandrum sativum leaves in large quantities early morning after dew evaporation for maximum oil content.
Wash the collected leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris then air dry them completely.
Steam distill the dried coriandrum sativum leaves using a clevenger apparatus or equivalent equipment at 100 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container after filtering it through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Store the obtained coriandrum sativum essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and refrigerate at 2-5 degrees celsius for prolonged shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with GERD because of its natural soothing and calming properties, which can help to relax the esophageal sphincter and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The oil's cooling sensation also helps to ease symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion by reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes. Additionally, peppermint essential oils may help to slow down gastric emptying, giving the stomach more time to digest food properly, thus reducing the likelihood of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint essential oil's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn associated with GERD.
Digestive soothing properties: The menthol content in peppermint essential oil helps to calm digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and discomfort, which are often exacerbated by GERD.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of GERD such as burning and discomfort after eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for GERD as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the esophageal mucosa and reduce inflammation associated with acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase them from a local farmer's market weighing around 1 kilogram.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at low temperature for 12 hours to preserve their potency and aroma.
Blend the dried peppermint leaves with carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:4 ratio adding 200 milliliters of oil mixture to the blender.
Steep the oil mixture with peppermint leaves for 2 weeks shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate optimal extraction of essential oils from herbs.
Strain the oil mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the extracted essential oil into dark glass bottles.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat GERD with herbal essential oils is by combining Bergamot oil's natural digestive properties with Ginger oil's anti-inflammatory effects. Add a few drops of each to a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply topically to the abdomen after meals. Alternatively, inhale the blend through steam inhalation or diffuse it in the air to soothe digestive discomforts.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to GERD that are treated with herbal essential oils are acid reflux, heartburn, and digestive discomfort. Essential oils like peppermint, ginger, and fennel help alleviate symptoms by soothing the stomach lining, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. Lemon balm oil can also be used to calm upset stomachs and relieve cramps. These oils can be used topically or inhaled through aromatherapy to provide natural relief from digestive issues.
